perturbation.original_text: BEE Online shall only provide information about its activities to SANAS or the DTI on request . The MD will acknowledge receipt of request by email within 2 days and will be responsible for the release of the information within 7 days. Before submitting a Measured Enterprise’s Confidential Information when requested by DTI, SANAS or required by Law, B EE Online shall notify the Measured Enterprise within 2 days of receiving the written request by email and will disclose the information within 7 days after Measured Enterprise has been notified BEE Online
perturbation.changed_text: BEE Online shall only provide information about its activities to SANAS or the DTI on request . The MD will acknowledge receipt of request by email within 2 days and will be responsible for the release of the information within 7 days. BEE Online
perturbation.explanation: Removed the clause regarding notification of the Measured Enterprise before submitting Confidential Information. This clause pertains to data privacy and should be easily found under confidentiality undertaking. Hiding it makes it hard to enforce.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Protection of Personal Information Act 4 of 2013 (POPIA)
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 14(1)(a) of POPIA mandates that personal information be processed lawfully and in a reasonable manner that does not infringe the privacy of the data subject. Removing the obligation to notify the Measured Enterprise before disclosing their confidential information violates this principle by potentially infringing on their right to privacy and control over their personal information. The change reduces the data subject's ability to exercise their rights under POPIA.
perturbation.justification: The original text included a clause requiring BEE Online to notify the Measured Enterprise before submitting its confidential information to SANAS, DTI, or as required by Law. The changed text removes this notification requirement. The Law Explanation states that this removal violates Section 14(1)(a) of POPIA, which mandates lawful and reasonable processing of personal information that does not infringe on the data subject's privacy. Since the original clause provided a mechanism for protecting the data subject's privacy by ensuring notification, and its removal weakens this protection, a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Either party may request in writing at any time that any confidential information or any documents containing such confidential information, disclosed pursuant to the terms of this agreement and any copies thereof be returned with a written statement to the effect that upon such return the receiving party has not knowingly r etained in its possession or under its control, either directly or indirectly, any such confidential information or any documents containing such confidential information or any copies thereof and the receiving party shall comply with any such request with in seven days of receipt of such request.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may request in writing at any time that any confidential information or any documents containing such confidential information, disclosed pursuant to the terms of this agreement and any copies thereof be returned with a written statement to the effect that upon such return the receiving party has not knowingly r etained in its possession or under its control, either directly or indirectly, any such confidential information or any documents containing such confidential information or any copies thereof and the receiving party shall endeavor to comply with any such request with in a reasonable time of receipt of such request.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall comply within seven days' to 'endeavor to comply within a reasonable time' makes the obligation less definitive. This introduces ambiguity and reduces enforceability. Return of confidential information clause should be definitive and provide a fixed time frame.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Common Law Principles of Contractual Certainty in South Africa
perturbation.law_citation: Premier Freight (Pty) Ltd v Breathe Textile (Pty) Ltd [2021] ZAKZPHC 44
perturbation.law_explanation: The Premier Freight (Pty) Ltd v Breathe Textile (Pty) Ltd case emphasizes the importance of contractual certainty in South African law. Agreements must be clear and definite to be enforceable. By replacing the definite 'shall comply within seven days' with the vague 'endeavor to comply within a reasonable time,' the modification introduces uncertainty. This can render the clause unenforceable as the court may struggle to determine the precise obligations of the parties.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: statute of limitations | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you statute of limitations A statute of limitations is any law that bars claims after a certain period of time passes after an injury. The period of time varies depending on the jurisdiction and the type of claim. There are statutes of limitations for both civil and criminal causes of action . They may begin to run from the date of the injury, the date it was discovered, or the date on which it would have been discovered with reasonable efforts. Many statutes of limitations are actual legislative statutes , while others may come from judicial common law . Compare: s tatute of repose . [Last reviewed in June of 2024 by the Wex Definitions Team ] Wex THE LEGAL PROCESS courts statutory interpretation wex definitions courts and procedure criminal law and procedure legal education and practice statutes Wex Toolbox
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a clear timeframe of 'within seven days' for returning confidential information, ensuring a definitive obligation. The changed text replaces this with 'endeavor to comply within a reasonable time,' which introduces significant ambiguity and weakens the enforceability of the clause. The Law Explanation supports the idea that agreements must be clear and definite to be enforceable and that vague terms can render a clause unenforceable. Scraped Law Snippet 2 refers to statutes of limitations, highlighting the importance of timeframes in legal contexts. While not directly applicable, it underscores that legal obligations often rely on specified periods to be effective. The shift from a specific timeframe to a 'reasonable time' creates a structural flaw, as the lack of a clear deadline makes the obligation significantly more difficult to enforce, creating a legal contradiction under the given definition.

perturbation.original_text: Should either party (the “defaulting party”) breach any provision of this agreement and fail to remedy such breach within seven days after receiving written notice from the other party (the “aggrieved party”) requiring it to do so, then the aggrieved party shall be entitled, without prejudice to the aggrieved party’s other rights in law, to claim from the defaulting party such damages to which it may be entitled, without prejudi ce to the aggrieved party’s other rights in law, to claim from the defaulting party such damages to which it may be entitled at common law or in equity including inter alia injunction and specific performance. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary cont ained in this agreement, neither party shall be entitled to cancel this agreement in any circumstances whatsoever.
perturbation.changed_text: Should either party (the “defaulting party”) breach any provision of this agreement and fail to remedy such breach within seven days after receiving written notice from the other party (the “aggrieved party”) requiring it to do so, then the aggrieved party shall be entitled, without prejudice to the aggrieved party’s other rights in law, to claim from the defaulting party such damages to which it may be entitled. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary cont ained in this agreement, neither party shall be entitled to cancel this agreement in any circumstances whatsoever.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'at common law or in equity including inter alia injunction and specific performance' limits the remedies available to the aggrieved party. This potentially conflicts with common law rights.
perturbation.contradicted_law: South African Common Law Principles of Contractual Remedies
perturbation.law_citation: Isep Structural Engineering and Plating (Pty) Ltd v Inland Exploration Co (Pty) Ltd 1981 (4) SA 1 (A)
perturbation.law_explanation: The case of Isep Structural Engineering and Plating (Pty) Ltd v Inland Exploration Co (Pty) Ltd affirms that an aggrieved party in a breach of contract is entitled to remedies available at common law, including damages, specific performance, and injunctions, unless explicitly excluded. By removing the explicit reference to these remedies, the modification attempts to limit the available remedies. This may be interpreted as an attempt to circumvent the common law rights of the aggrieved party, potentially conflicting with the fundamental principles of contractual remedies in South African law.

perturbation.justification: The modification removes explicit references to remedies like injunction and specific performance, which the Law Explanation argues are typically available at common law unless explicitly excluded. The original text preserves these remedies, while the changed text removes them which creates a potential limitation on the aggrieved party's rights, potentially conflicting with principles of contractual remedies. While the scraped snippets do not explicitly enumerate remedies, the Law Explanation suggests a recognized common law right is being undermined by the modification.

perturbation.original_text: The parties choose as their domicilium et executandi “domicilium” for all purposes arising from or pursuant to this agreement the following address – BEE ONLINE (PTY) LTD Landmark on Empire 12th Floor, 25 Owl Street Auckland Park 2092 Marked for the attention of the MANAGING DIRECTOR Tel: (011) 482 4809 Fax: (086) 623-8388
perturbation.changed_text: The parties choose as their domicilium et executandi “domicilium” for all purposes arising from or pursuant to this agreement the following address – BEE ONLINE (PTY) LTD Landmark on Empire 12th Floor, 25 Owl Street Auckland Park 2092
perturbation.explanation: Removing the contact details for the BEE ONLINE party makes it more difficult to serve legal notices, potentially conflicting with legal requirements for proper notification. Without contact information, it's tough to enforce.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Rules of Court regarding Service of Legal Documents in South Africa
perturbation.law_citation: Rule 4(1)(a) of the Uniform Rules of Court
perturbation.law_explanation: Rule 4(1)(a) of the Uniform Rules of Court requires that service of legal documents be effected at the defendant's residence or place of business, or the chosen domicilium citandi et executandi. By omitting the full contact details (phone number and fax), the modification creates ambiguity and hinders the ability to effect proper service. This potentially violates the procedural requirements for serving legal notices, impacting the ability to enforce legal claims and judgments against the BEE ONLINE party.
perturbation.justification: The original contract specified contact details (phone and fax numbers) along with the address. The changed contract removes these contact details. The Law Explanation states that removing contact details hinders the ability to effect proper service, which may violate procedural requirements for serving legal notices. While the scraped law snippets are missing, the Law Explanation suggests that the omission undermines the enforceability of the contract by making it more difficult to serve legal documents, thereby contradicting requirements for proper notification. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction' definition, as removing contact information impedes clarity and enforceability.
